items.title: Lakers used the bounty they wanted to send for Mark Williams, plus another 1st + swap, to get Walker Kessler. It's a STEEP price to pay but Lakers get him on a good contract. He'll be worth 19.7% of the cap in 2026-27 if it's a flat deal or 17.6% if it's escalating. That's far fr
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Lakers used the bounty they wanted to send for Mark Williams, plus another 1st + swap, to get Walker Kessler. It's a STEEP price to pay but Lakers get him on a good contract. He'll be worth 19.7% of the cap in 2026-27 if it's a flat deal or 17.6% if it's escalating. That's far from 25% rookie max.

items.title: Just under $13M AAV for Mark Williams is fair value. Good business for him and the Suns - not a super long or expensive deal to stunt Khaman Maluach’s growth, but also locks in their best option to start at C next season. 15 players under contract now including Bouyea/Highsmith

items.title: If Mark Williams' new contract with the Phoenix Suns follows the standard 8% raise structure, it will look like this:
items.author: KeithSmithNBA
items.body_excerpt:
If Mark Williams' new contract with the Phoenix Suns follows the standard 8% raise structure, it will look like this:

26-27: $11.7M
27-28: $12.7M
28-29: $13.6M

Phoenix projects to be $5.1M under their second-apron hard cap. The Suns currently have all 15 standard roster spots filled.

items.title: Jordan Goodwin had Early Bird rights with Suns because he was a waiver claim, so start of contract with Lakers counted toward his two years. Deal is for ~exact amount of tax MLE but Suns still have it available; could possibly use it, re-sign Mark Williams, and still juuuust limb
items.author: John Hollinger
items.body_excerpt: Jordan Goodwin had Early Bird rights with Suns because he was a waiver claim, so start of contract with Lakers counted toward his two years. Deal is for ~exact amount of tax MLE but Suns still have it available; could possibly use it, re-sign Mark Williams, and still juuuust limbo under 2nd apron.

The Phoenix Suns have tendered qualifying offers to Mark Williams and Koby Brea, a league source told @spotrac.com.

Williams and Brea will now be restricted free agents this offseason.
